This stage is the end-to-end verification layer for the MCP server executable. Rather than testing internal functions, it sits outside the binary and drives the real `codex-mcp-server` process through its JSON-RPC interface, confirming that startup, request handling, model interaction, and tool behavior work together as shipped.

`mcp-server/tests/all.rs` provides the single integration-test entry point, while `mcp-server/tests/suite/mod.rs` gathers the concrete test groups currently centered on `codex_tool`. Shared support lives under `tests/common`: `lib.rs` exposes reusable helpers, including decoding generic JSON-RPC results into typed values; `mcp_process.rs` launches the server as a child process and sends and receives line-delimited JSON-RPC messages; `mock_model_server.rs` stands in for the model backend with ordered, wiremock-served SSE `/v1/responses` streams; and `responses.rs` constructs those canned streams for scenarios like assistant text, shell invocations, and `apply_patch` requests. Using that infrastructure, `suite/codex_tool.rs` validates full codex-tool flows, especially approval prompts for shell commands and patches and correct forwarding of base and developer instructions.
